messages.content: Hi gpt do i have internet access?

I am an AI language model created by OpenAI, and as such, I don't have access to your devices or network, so I cannot determine whether you currently have internet access. However, if you are able to send this question and receive this answer, it implies that you have some form of internet access at this moment. If you're unsure about your connection status, you can check by trying to visit a website or perform an internet speed test. If you're on a smartphone or computer, you can also check your network settings to see if you're connected to a network and if the network has internet access.
No i mean can u assess internet?

As an AI developed by OpenAI, I don't have the ability to access the internet or browse the web in real-time. I am designed to provide information, answer questions, and engage in conversation based on the data I was trained on up until my last update. My knowledge is static and limited to what was included in my training data, with a cutoff in 2023.

If you have questions or need information on a topic, feel free to ask, and I'll do my best to provide a useful response based on the knowledge I have been trained with. However, for the most current information or live data, you would need to access the internet through a web browser or another online service.
Ok. I need a help regarding something about a mivie

Of course, I’d be happy to help with information about a movie! Please tell me what you're looking for, whether it's details about a specific film, help finding a movie title, information about cast and crew, general trivia or something else related to movies.

This is a Jackie chan movie. And this movie has a title song where jackie chan and some people are singing song in a bar or something. And during the middle of the song some people come to attack them. It is very old. Probably 1980s or 90s, idk. And this title song has the word "riches" in it. Can you identify the song or movie or can u just give some guesses.

The movie you're referring to appears to be "Wheels on Meals" (original title "Kuai can che"), which is a 1984 Hong Kong martial arts action-comedy film featuring Jackie Chan. In the movie, there's a famous scene set in a bar where a song plays with the lyrics that include the phrase "High upon high" which is often mistakenly heard as "high up in riches." This could be the song you're thinking of.

The scene is well-known because it's not just a song in a bar; it's a scene where a fight breaks out during the performance. Jackie Chan, along with co-stars Yuen Biao and Sammo Hung, ends up fighting off the attackers, combining humor and impressive martial arts skills—a common theme in Jackie Chan's movies from that era.

It's important to note that "Wheels on Meals" is known for its climactic fight scene between Jackie Chan and Benny "The Jet" Urquidez, which is often cited as one of the greatest fight scenes in martial arts movie history.

If this doesn't seem to be the movie you're thinking of, any additional details you can provide would be helpful in identifying the correct film and song.
Jackie chan might not be a main character.

Based on your description, there's a possibility that the movie you're recalling is "Dragons Forever" (1988). Jackie Chan is a main character but shares a nearly equal spotlight with his co-stars Sammo Hung and Yuen Biao. While the movie itself doesn't have a bar scene that matches exactly what you described regarding the title song with "riches" in it, it’s worth mentioning due to the collaborative nature of the trio’s films.

Another possibility is that it could be a scene from one of the many films where Jackie Chan has appeared, not necessarily as the main character. Jackie has had an extensive film career with roles in over 100 films, and the specifics can sometimes blend together or be hard to recall exactly.

There's also a chance that the film is not as internationally well-known or it might be one of his lesser-known works from his early career in Hong Kong cinema.

If "Dragons Forever" and "Wheels on Meals" are not the movies you're looking for, I would need more specifics to narrow it down. Could you provide any additional details about the scene, other actors, specific plot points, or even fragments of the lyrics? This information would greatly help in identifying the movie and song.
I'm sure the movie was just beginning. And people including jackie chan is singing it infront of a mic.

It sounds like you might be referring to the film "The Protector" (1985), which is a Hong Kong-American action film starring Jackie Chan and Danny Aiello. The movie opens with a scene in a bar where Jackie Chan's character is celebrating with fellow police officers. A fight breaks out shortly afterward. However, I'm not sure if there's a song involving "riches" or Jackie Chan singing into a mic during this scene.

Another possibility to consider is "Miracles" (also known as "The Canton Godfather," original title "Qi ji") from 1989, which is a Hong Kong action-comedy film directed by and starring Jackie Chan. The film is set in the 1930s and has several scenes with musical performances, and it's known for its period setting and elaborate sets. I should note, though, that while Jackie Chan's character gets involved in action sequences during the film, the specific bar scene you're describing with the singing does not immediately come to mind.

Jackie Chan has been known to sing the theme songs for many of his movies, so it could also be a lesser-known film where he provided the vocals for the theme or participated in a musical number.

If none of these suggestions match, the movie you're thinking of might be more obscure or the details mixed up. Any additional information would be helpful for a more accurate identification.

Remove Jackie chan from the point. It is a hongkong based movie. Movie start with a song. The song has riches in it. It is in a bar or similar set up. Some people are infront of the mic singing it. Suddenly some people came and attacked.

Thank you for clarifying. One movie that fits the description quite closely is "My Lucky Stars" (1985), which is part of the "Lucky Stars" series. The film is a Hong Kong action comedy and stars Sammo Hung, Jackie Chan, Yuen Biao, and other popular Hong Kong actors.

There is a scene early in the film where several characters are in a bar, and there's a performance happening. The characters are undercover, and the scene takes place in Japan, not Hong Kong. As they are singing, a brawl breaks out, which is a common trope in these types of films.

However, I am not aware of a specific song with the word "riches" in it from "My Lucky Stars" or another movie from that era that specifically matches all the details. Information about this film's soundtrack online is somewhat limited, so this could still be the film you're remembering if not all the details are exact.

Hong Kong cinema, particularly from the '80s and '90s, is well-known for blending genres, and it's not uncommon to have scenes where an upbeat moment quickly turns into a fight scene. If "My Lucky Stars" does not sound like the right movie, any additional context about the scene, characters, or lyrics would be beneficial in narrowing down the specific film.
What is that song in my lucky star

"My Lucky Stars" is a classic action-comedy film, part of a series that stars a group of martial artists and actors including Sammo Hung, Jackie Chan, and Yuen Biao. Although the film features some memorable scenes, including humorous and action-packed moments, there's no widely recognized song associated directly with the film that contains the word "riches" or is central to a bar scene, as you've described.

The confusion might arise from the tendency for these films to blend action and humor, and though there may be scenes with background music or performances, specific songs from "My Lucky Stars" do not stand out in the way iconic theme songs do in some of Jackie Chan's other films.

It's possible that the song you're referring to may be a lesser-known piece or not actually part of the "Lucky Stars" series. Without additional details, it remains challenging to pinpoint the exact film or song.

If you still believe the song is from "My Lucky Stars" or another movie from the same era, you might want to review the film or its soundtrack directly, as the information online about specific songs from these films can be quite limited.

Given the provided details, the film that comes closest to your description that I can think of might be "City Hunter" (1993). "City Hunter" is a Hong Kong action-comedy film directed by Wong Jing, starring Jackie Chan as the main character, Ryu Saeba. Despite Jackie Chan being a central character, the beginning of the movie does indeed feature a song number in a setting similar to a bar or club, which is typical of HK cinema from that era. However, it's important to remember that "City Hunter" is not from the 1980s but rather the early 1990s, and I cannot confirm a song with the lyrics "riches" in it. 

Given the nature of your question and the challenge to pinpoint an exact movie with the details provided, it's possible that your memory might be blending elements from various films. With '80s and '90s Hong Kong action cinema's penchant for lively opening sequences and sudden fight scenes, this event could easily have occurred in a number of films.

If "City Hunter" or none of the previously mentioned films is the right match, I would recommend looking up notable Hong Kong filmographies from the '80s and '90s to see if any titles jog your memory, or even watching some classic Jackie Chan or similar martial arts films from that period to locate the particular scene. Unfortunately, without more specific details, it's quite challenging to determine the exact film or song.
